seoTitle: West Toodyay vs Westminster — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between West Toodyay and Westminster. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: West Toodyay vs Westminster: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Toodyay and Westminster.
heading: West Toodyay vs Westminster — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Toodyay, Western Australia and Westminster, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Toodyay has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Westminster (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Toodyay or Westminster safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Toodyay has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Westminster scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Toodyay scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Westminster scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Toodyay or Westminster?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Toodyay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
